best: Skip CockroachDialect on a strftime test with the specific reason that experimental_strftime(time,string) has an unknown signature.
Questions newly-added explicit cleanup in several test classes since data is already cleaned up after each test, and challenges whether a removed @SkipForDialect is truly safe to drop.
none
Rather than @SkipForDialect for var_pop, use @RequiresDialectFeature with definesFunction(dialect,"var_pop") so the skip tracks actual dialect capability instead of being hard-coded.

Multiple ILike test skips are wrong: instead of @SkipForDialect the dialect should return false from supportsCaseInsensitiveLike() so the built-in emulation runs and the tests still pass — the skip masks a fixable dialect gap.
Odd that JsonMappingTests passes on MySQL without double-backslash quoting yet JsonJavaTimeMappingTests requires it — flags a likely escaping inconsistency in the test expectation.
Extract a shared base test class so the same entity mappings and assertions run across all temporal-table strategies, ensuring coverage parity rather than divergent per-strategy duplicates.
none

The proposed test reuse of ScriptTestCase only passes due to test execution order: its @BeforeClass ServerConfigurator.configure() expects a fully started server, but -v/-h never start one, so a dedicated parameterized test class is needed.

High-level installation-manager command tests should be integration tests (in manualmode/domain InstallationManagerIntegrationTestCase), not unit tests — unit tests are reserved for management resource operations.
Flags a duplicated assertion case in InstMgrResourceTestCase (line 836 repeats the previous one).
Replace Thread.sleep(2000) with branched ServerReload handling for the sec-mgr-enabled (server stops) vs disabled (reload succeeds) cases to make post-reload server state deterministic.

The Windows file-lock failure stems from cleanupSecurityManagerSubsystem reassigning a local client reference instead of the class field, so tearDown's TestLogHandlerSetupTask runs with a stale client.
The Windows lock occurs because the server is in admin-only mode at teardown so logHandlerSetup.tearDown() doesn't release file resources; add ServerReload before tearing down the log resources.
none

If PowerShell is unavailable on Windows the launchCommand assertion is silently skipped and the test reports green without verifying anything; it must check PowerShell availability (or use a JDK API).
On Windows without PowerShell the if-block is skipped so the test passes green while never asserting launchCommand; add a dedicated @Test that asserts the WFCORE-5917 verification assumption.
Guard the PowerShell Assume with isWindows() so the test is only reported as ignored on Windows-without-PowerShell, not on Linux where WFCORE-5917 needn't be verified.
This snapshot-save test should likely be excluded from the Bootable JAR test set since saving a snapshot is not a valid operation in a Bootable JAR.
none
none

The noRequestTimeout parameter is unused in the method — it should be applied at line 122 or removed; also timeouts should go through TimeoutUtil.adjust() for slower CI, consistently across all timeouts not just one.
